name: Glycine
historicalUse: Glycine-rich foods — particularly bone broth, cartilage, and collagen-containing preparations — have been used medicinally across virtually every traditional medical system. Rambam's dietary writings specifically recommend bone marrow and gelatin preparations for convalescence and weakness. Chinese medicine has used bone broth-based formulas for centuries. Glycine was isolated from gelatin in 1820 by Henri Braconnot, but its presence in therapeutic foods was empirically recognized long before.
modernEvidence: The GlyNAC combination (glycine + N-acetyl cysteine) has emerged as one of the most promising longevity interventions in recent clinical research. Dr. Rajagopal Sekhar's landmark trials at Baylor College of Medicine demonstrated that GlyNAC supplementation in older adults corrects glutathione deficiency, improves mitochondrial function, reduces oxidative stress, and improves muscle strength, gait speed, and cognitive function. Glycine itself is essential for glutathione (GSH) synthesis as one of its three precursor amino acids.
mechanisms: Glutathione precursor — one of three amino acids required for GSH synthesis (with cysteine and glutamate)
Collagen synthesis — obligate component of collagen triple helix (Gly-X-Y repeat)
Inhibitory neurotransmitter — glycine receptor agonist in spinal cord and brainstem, promoting muscle relaxation
Mitochondrial support — required for heme synthesis and electron transport chain components
Sleep quality — glycine receptor activation in the brain promotes deeper sleep stages
benefits: Supports glutathione synthesis and antioxidant defense
Supports collagen production and connective tissue integrity
Supports healthy aging and mitochondrial function
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. GRAS status. Well tolerated at therapeutic doses (3–15g/day). Mild sedation possible — take in evening if this is a concern.
contraindications: No known absolute contraindications at dietary supplement doses
drugInteractions: Clozapine — high-dose glycine may reduce clozapine efficacy in schizophrenia treatment; separate protocols
No clinically significant interactions at standard supplement doses
physicianNotes: The GlyNAC protocol (glycine 1.33mg/kg + NAC 0.81mg/kg) from Sekhar et al. represents a validated longevity intervention with strong RCT evidence. Glycine alone has significant sleep quality benefits at 3g before bed. Appropriate across a wide age range with particular benefit in patients over 40.
patientGuidance: Glycine powder has a mildly sweet taste and mixes easily in water or juice. If taken in the evening, it may improve your sleep quality. This is a normal and beneficial effect.
